Dear Nrsf. .Coyle: 
RE: 
Your Request for Informal 
Assistance 
Our File No. 1-89-344 
You have requested assistance concerning the campc,ign 
provisions of the Political Reform Act.ll 
Because we do not have 
sufficient facts to provide a specific answer to your request, we 
treat your letter as a request for informal assistance, rather 
than formal advice.21 
I hope the following general guidelines will be of assistance 
to you. 
You have ~r6vided a copy of a newsletter recently published 
in Modesto. 
The newsletter discusses Measure S, which was voted 
upon in a June special election. 
You have'been asked whether the 
person publishing the newsletter has incurred any campaign 
disclosure obligations. 
section 82025 defines the term "expenditure" as "a paynent, 
3-
forgiveness of a loan, a payment of a loan by a third party, or a~ 
enforceable promise to make a payment, unless it is clear from the 
surrounding circumstances that it is not made for political 
purposes ... " 
Regulation 18225 further defines the term "political 
purposes." 
In the case of a newsletter, an expenditure for 
political purposes is made if the communication "expressly 
advocates" the nomination, election or defeat of a candidate ... 
contains express words of advocacy such as "vote for," "elect," 
"support," "cast your ballot," "vote against," "defeat," "reject," 
or taken as a whole, unambiguously urges a particular result in a~ 
election ... " 
II Government Code Sec~ions 81000-91015. 
All statutory references 
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. 
Co~~issio~ 
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations section 
18000, et se~. 
All references to regulations are to Title 2, 
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations. 
,

in; ilunit.y·,-,· :.ded r.! an opinion or formal written advice.

; R,!gulation 18329 (c) (3).) 
428 J Street, Suite G~ } (;) P.O. Box 807 Q Sacramento CA 95804~0807 0 (916) 322,5660

::~ect:i.nn 18225(4) (C) 
stab:~r>: 
Notwithstanding the provisions of this subsection, the term 
expenditure does not include costs incurred for 
communications which expressly advocate the nomination, 
election or defeat of a clearly identified candidate or 
candidates or the qualification, passage or defeat of a 
clearly identified measure or measures by a regularly 
publjshed newsletter or regularly published 
peribdical ... whose circulation is limited to an 
organization's members, employees, shareholders, other 
affiliated individuals and those who request or purchase the 
publication. 
This paragraph applies only to the costs 
regularly incurred in publishing and distributing the 
newsletter or periodical. 
If additional costs are incurred 
because the newsletter or periodical is issued on other than 
its regular schedule, expanded in circulation, or 
substantially altered in style, size or format, the 
additional costs are expenditures. 
I have enclosed copies of the followin~ FPPC advice letters 
vlhich address costs incurred for publishing newsletters, 
periodicals, etc., and whether or not the costs are expenditures 
for political purposes and therefore required to be reported: 
John C. Petry, A-76-092; Jim Warren, A-86-120; Michael G. 
Loeffler, A-86-247i Greg Taber, A-88-285. 
If you have additional questions about the foregoing, please 
contact me at 916/322-5662. 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
-W\;;~~ C~

CITY of MODESTO 
Office of City Clerk: 
(209) 577-5396 
Ms. Mary Ann Kvasager 
801 11th Street, P. O. Box 642, Modesto, CA 95353 
(TOO (209) 526-9211 Hearing and Speech Impaired only] 
May 30, 1989 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Technical Assistance and Analysis Division 
P. O. Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 95804 
Dear Mary Ann: 
Would you mind referring another question to the proper person for me? 
11m not sure who to contact. 
Enclosed is a new "news1etter", "Check and Ba1ance," which has recently 
arrived on the scene. Although there is no "editor" or "writer" shown 
anyplace, there is an address. 
People have asked me if whoever this is has to report funds collected and 
expenditures, since they are asking people to subscribe, and because they are 
suggesting how to vote on Measure S, a measure for which we are having a 
special election on June 6, 1989. 
By the way, I didn't threaten anyone with jail. Mr. Munoz, referred to 
in the last paragraph, and I are getting along fine. 
I've helped him with all 
of his filings, etc. The newspaper quqted m~ correctly, but this newsletter 
doesn't quote the Bee article correctly. 
I'll appreciate it if you'd get this to whoever handles this kind of 
question. 
Thanks again, Mary Ann, for all of your help in the past. I don't know 
what I'd do without you. 
Sincerely, 
~ 
Norri ne Coyl e 
City Clerk & Auditor

WELCOME TO CHECK AND BALANCE 
CHECK AND BALANCE is a monthly newsletter that will provide you with the necessary information 
to evaluate your local government. You are entitled to the best possible service for your tax dol/ar. 
That is not going to happen unless you have facts which will enable you to make those decisions 
which will protect your tax dol/ar. 
Information is a two way street. CHECK AND BALANCE will do its level best to provide you with 
information you do not have access to otherwise, but in return, we need you to provide us with your 
experiences with local government. We need your Jetters. As important as your input is, we also 
need you to make CHECK AND BALANCE bigger and better. by becoming a Charter Subscriber .

uring the 1985 Modesto City Council election, then can-
date, Cannen Sabatino, wanted to protect our city serv-

erature. 
"By pursuing special interest projects, the city has 
ill tq cut corners by buying used fire equipment and 
!laying for years the expansion of police facilities meant to 
!Uer protect the citizens of Modesto. And it won't end

mvention center, or maybe the quality of service will just

:ople who protect us have the best equipment and facilities 
)ssible." 
!rhaps you remember the full page ads in the Modesto Bee 
and fire facilities, because the City of Modesto has spent 
over $29 million for the convention center. 
They have fonned a group called Safety First and have 
budgeted over $40,000 to convince you that unless you tax 
yourself you might not be safe in Modesto. Can you believe 
that? The Modesto Police Chief has stated that the money 
from Measure S will not put one additional police officer on 
the street. The fire department must wonder where opera-
tional money will come from if it is spent on facilities. 
Carmen Sabatino was right, we do need to suppon our police 
and fire department. However, do we want the same people 
who built the convention center to build the police and fire 
f;wilities? Do you want to give them over $30 million to 
spend? Will the City enter into negotiations with Allen 
Grant to build these facilities? If you fail to vote NO in this

P.O. Box 642 
Modesto, CA 
95353 
Dear Ms. Coyle: 
June 7, 1989 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-344 
Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform Act 
was received on June 5, 1989 by the Fair Political Practices 
commission. 
If you have any questions about your advice request, 
you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662. 
We try to answer all advice requests promptly. 
Therefore, 
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions, or 
more information is needed, you should expect a response within 21 
working days if your request seeks formal written advice. 
If more 
information is needed, the person assigned to prepare a response 
to your request will contact you shortly to advise you as to the 
information needed. 
If your request is for informal assistance, 
we will answer it as quickly as we can. 
(See Commission 
Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs. Sec. 18329).) 
You also should be aware that your letter and our response 
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon 
receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
ve.ry truly YOYJ2r
